title: Many's Many Simple Scales
description: An Origins layer for scales/sizes, but different than the other ones.
body:
## What's this?

Many's Many Simple Scales! It's our spin on an Origins player scale layer, intended to be used with a set of Origins that do *not* already scale the player - We prefer [Origins Minus](https://modrinth.com/mod/origins-minus). Also best used with both [Halfdoors](https://modrinth.com/mod/half-doors) and [Dramatic Doors](https://modrinth.com/mod/dramatic-doors).

8 player scales (including the default) are available from 0.25x (0.45 blocks tall) to 1.55x (2.79 blocks).

## Why, though?

After looking at the alternatives, mainly [Origins Player Scale](https://modrinth.com/datapack/origins-player-scale) and [Tinkerer's Statures](https://modrinth.com/mod/tinkerers-statures), I had a bit of a goldilocks issue. Tinkerer's Statures has good descriptions for what its scales do, and doesn't affect gameplay too much, but Origins Player Scale has more variety. I still suggest you look at that mod & datapack, and check if they're what *you* want! I learned a lot from looking at them and do think they're valuable and neat.

Basically, I wanted to make something that had:
- Catchy cute names for scales (US Imperial volume measurements, from teaspoon to gallon, with cup as the default)
- Icons and descriptions that place things relative to mobs in the game
- A wide range of scales, while still being playable.
- Almost no mechanics affected (ie, you move at the same speeds, have normal player reach and such. Gallon sized players do walk up 1-block ledges automatically, though.)
- Descriptions that state the sneaking and standing heights of every size.

## The list of sizes:

- Teaspoon (0.25x, 0.45 blocks tall)
- Tablespoon (0.5x, 0.9 blocks tall)
- Ounce (0.65x, 1.17 blocks tall)
- Gill (0.8x, 1.44 blocks tall)
- Cup (Default)
- Pint (1.15x, 2.07 blocks tall)
- Quart (1.33x, 2.394 blocks tall)
- Gallon (1.55x, 2.79 blocks tall)
